lyrics:
I left you a note on the nightstand last night
Just some lines that I scribbled, they didn't come out right
Something about missing you, something about time
I don't know, it made sense when it ran through my mind

I thought of a necklace, a ring, or a song
Something small you could hold while I'm gone
But nothing felt perfect, so I let it slide
Figured you'd know how I feel deep inside

A scattershot forget-me-not, I love the way that sounds
A half-written postcard, some words that I lost and found
I don't know why I say these things, but I hope you understand
That if I could wrap up forever, I'd place it in your hands

Tried to tell you this morning, but the words came out strange
Said, "I'll call when I can," but that's not what I meant
I mean, "Don't let me fade, don't let us change"
But I guess all my meanings get rearranged

A scattershot forget-me-not, yeah, maybe that's just me
A melody tangled in everything I don't say clearly
I don't know why I ramble on, but I hope you feel it too
That if I could leave you forever, I'd leave it all with you

Maybe I'm bad at goodbyes, maybe I'm worse at holding on
Maybe my love's just a puzzle with half the pieces drawn
But you always filled in the rest… you always know me best

A scattershot forget-me-not, I guess that's what I am
A handful of moments I send in the wind
A love that I fumble, my song out of tune
But if you ever feel lonely… just hum it. Will you
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track has an intimate and reflective texture, built around a warm, felt-like piano and a present, breathy male vocal that creates a tender, heartfelt atmosphere. The energy level is a slow build, moving from a quiet, sparse verse to a more open, emotionally resonant chorus with a gentle, flowing ballad feel. The production quality is clean and spacious, allowing the lead vocal to sit comfortably in front of the mix while layers of subtle percussion and swelling strings add density without cluttering the arrangement. A defining quality of the track is the dynamic swell into the chorus, where the added instrumentation elevates the song from a simple personal reflection to a more powerful, cinematic statement.

review.review_text: The title earns its keep: 'scattershot forget-me-not' is the kind of phrase that makes you rewind, and PCBender builds the whole song honestly around that idea, a love letter from someone who keeps fumbling the words but means every one of them. In E major at a clip that feels faster on paper than it does in the room, the track leans on a warm piano and a vocal that stays close and unguarded through the verses before the strings open things up at the chorus. The bridge lands the emotional gut-punch quietly: 'maybe my love's just a puzzle with half the pieces drawn / but you always filled in the rest.' A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'maybe my love's just a puzzle with half the pieces drawn / but you always filled in the rest'
